title: Pleurospermum rotundatum

intro: Pleurospermum rotundatum, also known as Round-leaved Pleurospermum or Mountain Carrot, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its yellow flowers and rounded, lobed leaves. Traditionally, the plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Pleurospermum rotundatum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive discomfort.
